title: Playtest Closure, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, and Production Defense · Language Clinic: Release Gates
summary: A polished demo becomes release by copying the latest executable; no one owns accessibility findings, performance budgets, package provenance, crash recovery, rollback, or support. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the release gates increment in production game release and defense dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…
objectives: Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.
Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Predict Release Gates · Theory Release Gates
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 8540 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Release Gates contract for the supplied data.

Apply that move specifically to Release Gates while producing production game release and defense dossier.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 8540 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch. A defensible solution applies Release Gates, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to production game release and defense dossier.

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-release-gates — Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-release-recovery-defense-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-release-recovery-defense-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_release_recovery_defense_3(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, performance: bool = True, portability: bool = True, security: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "player-quality"
    if not performance or not portability or not security:
        return False, passed, "release-quality"
    if not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "recovery-gap"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-game-defense"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A theory Asteroids team handles case 9536 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: If CI passes, the newest local executable is the release artifact.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch. Artifact under review: production game release and defense dossier.

options.text: For inspect release gates evidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Release Gates for production game release and defense dossier.
The record names the assumption, applies Release Gates, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Release Gates record says production game release and defense dossier looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect release gates evidence boundary for Release Gates.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Release Gates model · Theory Release Gates
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Release Gates: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 5393 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Release Gates model · Theory Release Gates.
steps.why: Retrieving Release Gates through the concrete production game release and defense dossier case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Release Gates model · Theory Release Gates, keep Release Gates connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Release Gates model · Theory Release Gates, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Release Gates precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Release Gates model · Theory Release Gates, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 5393 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Release Gates model · Theory Release Gates, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: If CI passes, the newest local executable is the release artifact.
